_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average price index of key commodities including coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice, stands at 141, representing a 41% increase since 2015. This rise signals a significant climb in costs compared to the 2015 baseline of 100. The increase has not been uniform across all items, reflecting recent and uneven price movements. The basket provides a composite view of these global soft commodity prices.
notes.movers: Coffee has been the largest driver, more than doubling with a 99% increase since 2015, followed by orange juice at a 34% rise. Wheat and sugar have grown more modestly, by 19% and 13% respectively. These price changes are largely attributed to weather events and crop diseases, such as frost and drought affecting coffee-producing arabica regions and citrus greening impacting orange juice supplies. Given that soft commodities trade in relatively thin markets, these factors can cause substantial volatility unrelated to broad inflation trends.
notes.why: The headline increase in the breakfast basket is primarily driven by a few weather-sensitive crops that are vulnerable to disease and climate impacts. Because these markets are tightly supplied and exposed to environmental fluctuations, prices can remain elevated for extended periods. Understanding these dynamics is crucial for interpreting soft commodity price developments.
